reasoning: The exact resolution dates are not listed, but given the close date of 2026-01-24, they must fall between late October 2025 and late January 2026. Alençon typically experiences a cooling trend during this period, with late October daily average temperatures around 10–12°C, dropping to 4–6°C by December and January. Assuming standard deviations of about 3–4°C for these daily means, the probability of a random day in late autumn/winter being warmer than a random day in late October is roughly equal to P(N(5, 4^2) > N(11, 3^2)), which yields a Z-score around 1.2 corresponding to ~11.5%. If multiple dates are aggregated via a logical AND ("higher on each... than on 2025-10-26"), the probability would be even lower, though heavily dependent on how cold October 26 turns out to be. Balancing a single resolution date vs. multiple, I estimate the probability at 0.12. *0.12*
